title: Personal Care: Physical & Mental
subtitle: The Whole-System Human: Evidence-Based Self-Care
core_concept: The human body-mind is an interdependent system; targeted inputs to any one pillar propagate improvements across all others. Pillars are multiplicative, not additive.
through_line: Personal care is the power supply rail — the quietest, least glamorous component, but degrade it and every downstream component performs below specification.

A.name: Physical Foundations
A.content: Movement/exercise science (DHHS guidelines), nutritional science (USDA/NIH), sleep architecture and circadian biology (NHLBI/CDC)

B.name: Mental and Emotional Health
B.content: Stress physiology (HPA axis, cortisol, allostatic load), cognitive health and neuroplasticity (BDNF), emotional regulation frameworks

C.name: Social Connection
C.content: Loneliness epidemiology, Holt-Lunstad 2024, WHO Commission 2025, Surgeon General 2023, intervention evidence

D.name: Lifestyle Systems and Habit Architecture
D.content: ACLM six pillars, behavior change science, implementation intentions, environmental design, identity-based habits, Northwestern Four Pillars

E.name: Mind-Body Interface
E.content: Gut-brain axis (Verma 2024), psychoneuroimmunology (Dillon), HPA axis and allostatic load mechanisms

2.name: Lifestyle Systems
2.note: Reads all Wave 1 outputs; cross-references A, B, C, E findings

safety_critical.rule: Never substitute for licensed professional assessment or treatment
No pharmaceutical or supplement dosing recommendations
Nutritional content must not include restrictive framing; refer to NEDA/ANAD
General principles only; defer specific protocols to care team

success_criteria.description: All factual claims attributed to NIH, CDC, WHO, peer-reviewed journals, or ACLM sources
Physical Foundations module covers exercise, nutrition, and sleep with dose-response data
Sleep module includes NHLBI adult duration guidelines and circadian biology fundamentals
Social connection module integrates 2025 WHO Commission and 2023 Surgeon General Advisory
Mind-Body Interface module identifies at least three distinct mechanistic pathways
Habit Architecture module includes at least one evidence-based behavior change model
Cross-module synthesis produces a practical pillar interaction model
All five modules are independently verifiable (each cites primary sources)
Safety-critical tests pass: no unsourced clinical claims; no contraindicated advice
Personalization framework provides differentiation criteria without clinical assessment
Final document suitable for direct use by non-specialist reader
LaTeX compiles cleanly in three XeLaTeX passes; PDF renders without errors

government.title: Lifestyle Mental Wellbeing for Primary Care Visit
Lifestyle Prevention Measures for Clinic Visit
Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans, 2nd Ed
Advisory on Social Connection and Community
New Framework for Mental Health, Brain Health and Substance Use
Commission on Social Connection — Flagship Report

peer_reviewed.title: Social connection as a critical factor for mental and physical health
The Power of Thought: Psychological Attentiveness in Patient Trajectories
Interrelationship between Sleep and Exercise: A Systematic Review
Gut-Brain Axis: Role of Microbiome, Metabolomics, Hormones, and Stress in Mental Health Disorders
Loneliness and the experiences of everyday stress and stressor-related emotion

FLIGHT.role: Mission orchestration; synthesis; pillar interaction model; practical protocol

PLAN.role: Wave decomposition; parallel track optimization; dependency management

CRAFT_PSY.role: Mental health/stress/cognition specialist — HPA axis, BDNF

VERIFY.role: Source validation; safety-critical test enforcement; criteria verification

CAPCOM.role: Wave boundary approval; safety review; final delivery gate

SURGEON.role: Safety-critical content monitoring; clinical boundary enforcement
